basic_info.title: Pokémon Emerald Version
basic_info.review: Pokémon Emerald Version is widely considered the definitive Generation III experience, offering a polished and expanded narrative that resolves the plot holes of Ruby and Sapphire.

play_today.details: The definitive original version with authentic hardware experience. Features the Battle Frontier, Rayquaza storyline, and all Gen III content. Best played on original GBA or GBA SP hardware for authenticity.

recommended_patches.name: Bugfix Patch
recommended_patches.description: Fixes various glitches and bugs in the original game including RNG issues and battle mechanics.

recommended_patches.name: RTC Clock Fix
recommended_patches.description: Resolves internal battery RTC issues that prevent clock-based events from functioning properly after save file corruption.

optimal_settings: Enable Real Time Clock in emulator settings
Set Save Type to Flash 128K
Use 2x or 3x internal resolution for sharper visuals
Enable fast-forward for battle speedup
Custom button mapping for touchscreen
Save state slots enabled for quick saving
Enable RTC support in settings

play_today.details: Modern emulation provides the most accessible way to play today with enhanced features like save states, fast-forward, and screen scaling. mGBA is the recommended emulator for best compatibility.

recommended_patches.name: Bugfix Patch
recommended_patches.description: Fixes various glitches and bugs in the original game including RNG issues and battle mechanics.

recommended_patches.name: Hoenn Dex Edition
recommended_patches.description: Allows obtaining all Hoenn region Pokémon without trading, ideal for completionists and Nuzlocke runs.

recommended_patches.name: National Dex Edition
recommended_patches.description: Further expanded version allowing all Gen 3 Pokémon without trading requirements.

optimal_settings: Enable Real Time Clock in emulator settings
Set Save Type to Flash 128K
Use 2x or 3x internal resolution for sharper visuals
Enable fast-forward for battle speedup
Use mGBA core instead of other GBA cores
Enable shader for CRT effect if desired
Configure save state slots
Custom button mapping for touchscreen
Save state slots enabled
Enable RTC support in settings

play_today.platform: Game Boy Advance (3DS Virtual Console Injection)
play_today.details: Not officially available on 3DS Virtual Console. Requires homebrew injection for 3DS play, which may have RTC compatibility issues and is not recommended for new players.

recommended_patches.name: RTC Fix Patch
recommended_patches.description: Essential patch for 3DS injection to fix RTC clock issues that commonly occur with injected GBA games.

optimal_settings: Enable Real Time Clock in emulator settings
Set Save Type to Flash 128K
Use 2x or 3x internal resolution for sharper visuals

play_today.details: The European version is functionally identical to the North American version but may have slight text differences. It is a viable option if you prefer the European release date or specific regional content. However, it lacks the widespread community support and patch availability of the North American version. Emulation remains the best way to play for convenience and modern features.

recommended_patches.name: Pokémon Emerald DX
recommended_patches.description: A comprehensive quality-of-life patch for the Game Boy Advance version that adds modern conveniences like a Pokédex search function, battle log, and improved UI scaling without altering core gameplay.

recommended_patches.name: Pokémon Emerald Balanced Edition
recommended_patches.description: A Game Boy Advance patch that rebalances type matchups, move power, and encounter rates to create a more competitive and fair experience for the Hoenn region.

recommended_patches.name: All-Instrument Patch (Emerald)
recommended_patches.description: An audio enhancement patch for the Game Boy Advance version that expands the sound engine to include all instruments from the original soundtrack, improving music quality.

recommended_patches.name: Faster Emerald
recommended_patches.description: A speedrun and quality-of-life patch for the Game Boy Advance version that removes unnecessary dialogue delays and speeds up battle animations.

recommended_patches.name: Pokémon Emerald Enhanced QoL
recommended_patches.description: A Game Boy Advance patch focusing on user experience improvements, including better inventory management and streamlined menu navigation.

optimal_settings: Set internal resolution to 4x or 8x for crisp 4K upscaling
Enable 'Turbo' button mapping for rapid movement and menu navigation
Configure save states to auto-save every 5 minutes for safety
Use 'Bilinear' filtering for smoother sprite scaling on high DPI screens
Set resolution to 4x native for sharp visuals on modern phones
Enable touch controls with customizable turbo buttons
Activate cloud save sync for cross-device progress
Use 'Linear' texture filtering to reduce pixelation artifacts
Enable 4x internal resolution for high-DPI display support
Map turbo keys to 'Z' and 'X' for efficient gameplay
Use save states for quick checkpointing during difficult battles
Disable audio latency compensation for smoother sound

description.synopsis: In the Hoenn region, the player embarks on a journey to become a Pokémon Champion while uncovering a conspiracy involving two rival teams, Team Magma and Team Aqua. These teams seek to alter the world's climate by awakening the legendary Pokémon Groudon and Kyogre, threatening to destroy the balance of nature. The player must stop both teams and restore peace to the region.
key_features: Battle Frontier with 10 distinct challenge facilities
Expanded storyline featuring both Team Magma and Team Aqua
Showdown battles against the legendary Pokémon Groudon and Kyogre
Introduction of the Pokétch-like Battle Frontier Pass
Enhanced graphics with updated battle animations and weather effects
Expanded Pokédex with 202 Pokémon and new evolution methods
Double Battle mechanics integrated into the main story
Post-game content including the Battle Frontier and Deoxys event
legacy_and_impact: Pokémon Emerald Version is historically significant as the first 'enhanced' version in the series to include a substantial new post-game area (Battle Frontier) rather than just minor tweaks. It is frequently cited by fans and critics as the best mainline Pokémon game of the Game Boy Advance era and a high point for the franchise's storytelling depth.
